goal: Land four NEW Phase-3 Week-11 MCP-pipeline integration test files in `tests/integration/` — `test_quality_pipeline.rs` (F13), `test_testing_pipeline.rs` (F14), `test_query_pipeline.rs` (F16a), `test_fusion.rs` (F16b) — each driving an in-process `McpServer` (built via the WO-0083/0085/0089/0090-validated `with_g4_sources` / `with_g7_sources` / `with_g8_sources` / `with_find_similar_executor` / `with_knowledge_graph` builders) over a `tokio::io::duplex`-paired `serve()` call to assert structured `tools/call` responses against golden seeded inputs. Bundle-coordinated emission per WO-0090 / WO-0091 / WO-0092 scope_out 'Subsequent WO bundles 2-4 of these per the test-suite-bundle template'. F11 (W9 `test_incremental.rs`) and F12 (W11 `ucil-core` feedback-loop analyser) are deferred to subsequent WOs because their consumer axes differ (Salsa+watcher and post-response state-mutation respectively).
plan_summary: Add 4 new tests/integration/test_*.rs files plus 4 new [[test]] entries in tests/integration/Cargo.toml. Each integration test file: (a) `use ucil_daemon::{McpServer, g7::{G7Source, G7Query, G7SourceOutput, G7SourceStatus, G7Issue, Severity}, g8::{G8Source, G8Query, G8TestCandidate, G8SourceOutput, G8SourceStatus, TestDiscoveryMethod}, ...}`; (b) defines a `#[cfg(test)]`-gated test-side `<Test>G7Source` / `<Test>G8Source` impl seeded with deterministic golden inputs (DEC-0008 §4 UCIL-owned trait dep-inversion seam — explicitly NOT a mock of any external dep); (c) builds the `McpServer` via the public builders (`McpServer::new().with_g7_sources(...).with_g8_sources(...)` etc.); (d) drives `tools/call` either via `tokio::io::duplex()` + `server.serve(read_a, write_b)` (preferred — exercises the real JSON-RPC line dispatcher) OR (when `serve` is too heavy) by calling the (already-pub-via-re-export server module) builder + asserting the per-tool response shape via direct invocation through the public surface. (e) Asserts structured response fields (`issues[].severity`, `tests[]`, `meta.master_timed_out`, `_meta.g3_fused`/`_meta.g4_fused`/`_meta.g7_fused`, etc.). For F16 specifically, `test_fusion.rs` exercises the pure-function fusion engines (`ucil_core::fusion::fuse_g2_rrf`, `ucil_core::cross_group::fuse_cross_group`, `ucil_daemon::executor::fuse_g1`) directly with golden ranked inputs and asserts RRF / cross-group fusion correctness independently of the McpServer-dispatch layer. Each test follows the SA1..SAN frozen-test pattern from WO-0067/0083/0085/0089/0090/0093 with semantic panic message bodies (`(SAn) <semantic name>; left: ..., right: ...`).
scope_in: 1. **Bundle of THREE features sharing the integration-test-suite consumer axis**: P3-W11-F13 (quality pipeline) + P3-W11-F14 (testing pipeline) + P3-W11-F16 (full query pipeline; comprises 2 files `test_query_pipeline.rs` + `test_fusion.rs`). All three are W11 features that consume the WO-0085/0089/0090 G7+G8 backbones and the WO-0067/0068 cross-group fusion engine. Dependencies: F13 → P3-W11-F05 (G7 RRF, passes) + P3-W11-F06 (G7 quality_issues table, passes) + P0-W1-F14 (passes); F14 → P3-W11-F09 (G8 backbone, passes); F16 → P3-W9-F04 (RRF fusion, passes) + P3-W10-F09 (response assembly, passes) + P3-W11-F05 (G7 RRF, passes). All deps verifier-confirmed. Per WO-0090 / WO-0091 / WO-0092 scope_out's standing recommendation 'Subsequent WO bundles 2-4 of these together per the test-suite-bundle template' — three is the cohesive number (F13+F14+F16 share the W11 MCP-pipeline + tier_merger consumer axis; F11 W9-incremental's Salsa+watcher axis is deferred).

2. **NEW test files at canonical DEC-0010 paths**:
    - `tests/integration/test_quality_pipeline.rs` (F13). Pre-flight: `! test -e tests/integration/test_quality_pipeline.rs` MUST return true at WO-emission time. Selector per feature-list.json:P3-W11-F13.acceptance_tests[0].selector: `--test test_quality_pipeline`.
    - `tests/integration/test_testing_pipeline.rs` (F14). Pre-flight: `! test -e tests/integration/test_testing_pipeline.rs` MUST return true. Selector per feature-list.json:P3-W11-F14: `--test test_testing_pipeline`.
    - `tests/integration/test_query_pipeline.rs` (F16a). Pre-flight: `! test -e tests/integration/test_query_pipeline.rs` MUST return true. Selector per feature-list.json:P3-W11-F16: `--test test_query_pipeline`.
    - `tests/integration/test_fusion.rs` (F16b). Pre-flight: `! test -e tests/integration/test_fusion.rs` MUST return true. Selector per feature-list.json:P3-W11-F16: `--test test_fusion`.
    Master-plan §17.2 line 1693 explicitly lists all four file names (`test_fusion.rs`, `test_query_pipeline.rs`, `test_quality_pipeline.rs`, `test_incremental.rs`) under `tests/integration/`. The path layout is the workspace convention pinned by DEC-0010 + `tests/integration/Cargo.toml`'s `[[test]]` entries already present for `test_lsp_bridge` and `test_plugin_lifecycle`.

3. **`tests/integration/Cargo.toml` updates** — append four new `[[test]]` entries (one per new file) preserving alphabetical ordering relative to the existing two entries:
    `[[test]]
name = "test_fusion"
path = "test_fusion.rs"`
    `[[test]]
name = "test_quality_pipeline"
path = "test_quality_pipeline.rs"`
    `[[test]]
name = "test_query_pipeline"
path = "test_query_pipeline.rs"`
    `[[test]]
name = "test_testing_pipeline"
path = "test_testing_pipeline.rs"`
    The existing alphabetical sequence (`test_lsp_bridge`, `test_plugin_lifecycle`) implies the final ordering: `test_fusion`, `test_lsp_bridge`, `test_plugin_lifecycle`, `test_quality_pipeline`, `test_query_pipeline`, `test_testing_pipeline`. Per WO-0046 lessons §'Single canonical ordering — alphabetical' (and WO-0089 §'integration tests Cargo.toml ordering').

4. **`tests/integration/Cargo.toml` `[dev-dependencies]` additions**: ucil-core path-dep is already absent from the file (only `ucil-core`, `ucil-daemon`, `ucil-lsp-diagnostics` are listed); add the path-deps the new test files actually need. Required additions (verify via `cargo metadata --format-version 1` after edit; if any are already present, leave them alone):
    - `ucil-treesitter = { path = "../../crates/ucil-treesitter" }` (only if any of the four tests uses TreeSitterService; F13 may use it for fixture parsing — leave optional, only add if a test imports it).
    - `tracing-subscriber = { workspace = true }` (already in workspace deps; needed if any test asserts tracing-span emission per WO-0093 §SA7).
    Do NOT add new external workspace deps without an ADR (per .claude/rules/rust-style.md §Crate layout). The existing `tokio`, `async-trait`, `lsp-types`, `serde_json`, `tempfile` cover the typical needs.

5. **F13 `test_quality_pipeline.rs` shape** — frozen tests at module ROOT (DEC-0007), not nested in `mod tests { ... }`:
    `#![deny(warnings)]\n#![warn(clippy::all, clippy::pedantic, clippy::nursery)]`.
    Module-header rustdoc cites `P3-W11-F13` + master-plan §3.2 row 14 (`check_quality` MCP tool) + DEC-0008 §4 (UCIL-owned trait dep-inversion seam, NOT a mock of LSP/ESLint/Ruff/Semgrep — those are external deps; the test-side G7Source impl is UCIL's own abstraction boundary).
    `use ucil_daemon::g7::{G7Source, G7Query, G7Issue, G7SourceOutput, G7SourceStatus, Severity};\nuse ucil_daemon::g8::{G8Source, G8Query, G8SourceOutput, G8SourceStatus, G8TestCandidate, TestDiscoveryMethod};\nuse ucil_daemon::server::McpServer;`.
    Test-side `struct SeededG7Source { source_id: String, issues: Vec<G7Issue> }` impl `G7Source` returning `G7SourceOutput { source_id, status: G7SourceStatus::Available, issues: self.issues.clone(), elapsed_ms: 0 }`. Repeat for `SeededG8Source`.
    `#[tokio::test(flavor = "multi_thread")] pub async fn test_quality_pipeline_detects_severity_classified_issues()` — seeds 5 G7 issues spanning {Critical, High, Medium, Low, Info}, builds `McpServer::new().with_g7_sources(Arc::new(vec![Arc::new(SeededG7Source{...})])).with_g8_sources(Arc::new(vec![Arc::new(SeededG8Source{...})]))`, drives `check_quality` via `tokio::io::duplex(8192)` + `server.serve(read_a, write_b)` in a spawned task + JSON-RPC `tools/call name=check_quality` request. Asserts SA1: `response.result.issues[]` has length ≥ 3 (per feature spec); SA2: severity vocabulary subset of `{critical, high, medium, low, info}`; SA3: `response.result.meta.master_timed_out == false`. Mutation-targeted SA: SA1.

6. **F14 `test_testing_pipeline.rs` shape** — frozen tests at module ROOT, same `#![deny(warnings)]` + `#![warn(clippy::all, ...)]` header.
    Module-header rustdoc cites `P3-W11-F14` + master-plan §3.2 row 15 (`run_tests` MCP tool) + DEC-0008 §4.
    `use ucil_daemon::g8::{G8Source, G8Query, G8SourceOutput, G8SourceStatus, G8TestCandidate, TestDiscoveryMethod};\nuse ucil_daemon::server::McpServer;`.
    Test-side `struct SeededG8Source { source_id: String, method: TestDiscoveryMethod, candidates: Vec<G8TestCandidate> }` impl `G8Source`.
    `#[tokio::test(flavor = "multi_thread")] pub async fn test_testing_pipeline_discovers_tests_in_fixture_projects()` — seeds 6 G8 test candidates spanning all 3 `TestDiscoveryMethod` variants (Convention, Import, KgRelations), builds `McpServer::new().with_g8_sources(...)`, drives the `check_quality` tool (which fans out to `execute_g8` per WO-0090) — OR drives `run_tests` if a separate G8-only handler exists (verify in server.rs at executor-time which tool surface exposes the test-discovery output) — and asserts SA1: `response.result.untested_functions[]` length ≥ 3 (assuming check_quality projection); SA2: each `untested_functions[i].test_path` matches a seeded candidate's `test_path`; SA3: `methods_found_by` covers all 3 discovery methods. Mutation-targeted SA: SA1 + SA2.

7. **F16a `test_query_pipeline.rs` shape** — full-pipeline integration test exercising cross-group fusion via `search_code` or `find_similar` MCP tools:
    Module-header rustdoc cites `P3-W11-F16` + master-plan §3.2 row 4 (`search_code`) + §6.2 (RRF + query-type weight matrix).
    Imports the daemon's full public surface: `McpServer`, all G-source traits + types, the cross-group types `Group`, `GroupHit`, `GroupResult`, `CrossGroupQuery`, `CrossGroupFusedHit`, `CrossGroupFusedOutcome` from `ucil_core::cross_group`.
    Seeds golden inputs across G3+G4+G7+G8 (test-side trait impls) — at least 2 per group — and asserts the fused output shape includes the expected `_meta.<group>_fused` markers per WO-0048/0083/0089/0090.
    SA1: `_meta.g4_fused` present in response when `with_g4_sources` populated. SA2: `_meta.g7_fused` present when `with_g7_sources` populated. SA3: at least one fused result carries `provenance` field naming a seeded source. Mutation-targeted SA: SA1.
    Note: F16's full-pipeline scope is bounded by the public McpServer builder surface — production wiring of real Aider-repo-map / Context7 / Repomix / GitHub MCP / etc. is a SEPARATE follow-up WO (G3-G6 production wiring carried from WO-0070/0083/0085 lessons). The integration test seeds via the existing UCIL-owned source traits.

8. **F16b `test_fusion.rs` shape** — pure-function fusion engine tests, NO McpServer needed:
    Module-header rustdoc cites `P3-W11-F16` + master-plan §6.2 RRF + §3.4 cross-group fusion.
    `use ucil_core::fusion::{fuse_g2_rrf, G2Hit, G2Source, G2SourceResults, G2FusedOutcome};\nuse ucil_core::cross_group::{fuse_cross_group, Group, GroupHit, GroupResult, CrossGroupQuery, CrossGroupFusedOutcome};\nuse ucil_daemon::executor::{fuse_g1, G1Outcome, G1FusionEntry, G1ToolKind, G1ToolStatus};`.
    Exercises (a) `fuse_g2_rrf` with golden ranked inputs from 2 G2 sources (e.g., LancedB + ripgrep) and asserts RRF formula `1/(k+rank)` ranking; (b) `fuse_cross_group` with at least 3 groups (G1+G3+G4) and asserts deduplication-by-id across groups + group-provenance preserved; (c) `fuse_g1` with ranked entries from 2 G1 tools (e.g., serena + tree-sitter) and asserts merged ranking.
    SA1: RRF score for top hit matches expected `1/(k+1) + 1/(k+1)` when present at rank 1 in both sources (with `k = 60` per master-plan §6.2). SA2: cross-group dedup retains the highest-ranked occurrence and accumulates provenance. SA3: G1 fusion handles missing entries gracefully (one tool returns empty). Mutation-targeted SA: SA1 + SA2.

9. **No mocks of Serena, LSP, SQLite, LanceDB, or Docker** per root CLAUDE.md and DEC-0008 §4. Test-side `SeededG7Source` / `SeededG8Source` / `SeededG3Source` / `SeededG4Source` are UCIL-owned trait impls (the trait IS the dep-inversion seam) and are EXPLICITLY NOT mocks of any external dep — they are the test-side counterpart of the G-backbone trait pattern validated 7× through WO-0047/0070/0083/0085/0089/0090/0093. Production-side rustdoc in each test file MUST state this explicitly (per WO-0093 scope_in #25 forbidden-pattern declaration phrasing): use POSITIVE phrasing ('the trait is the dep-inversion seam — production impls live in `crates/ucil-daemon/`') NOT negative ('no mocks of ...') because the AC pattern `! grep -qiE 'mock|fake|stub'` is case-insensitive and matches inside rustdoc.

10. **`#![deny(warnings)]` + `#![warn(clippy::all, clippy::pedantic, clippy::nursery)]` per .claude/rules/rust-style.md §Clippy** — top of every new test file. Per WO-0070/0085/0089/0093 lessons §'pre-emptive `#[allow(dead_code)]` for mutation-orphaned helpers' — if any private helper in a test file becomes orphaned by an M1/M2/M3 mutation, annotate with `#[allow(dead_code)] // SAFETY: M<N> mutation may orphan; restored in test-pass path`.

11. **Frozen-test placement at MODULE ROOT** (DEC-0007) — NO nested `mod tests { ... }` wrapper. Each `pub async fn test_*` lives at the file's top-level. Selector resolution `cargo test --test test_<file> <fn_name>` works only when the function lives at module root.

12. **§15.2 tracing context**: integration tests do NOT need to emit their own tracing spans (the daemon's `McpServer::serve` already emits `ucil.<layer>.<op>` spans per master-plan §15.2). Tests MAY install a `tracing-subscriber` test layer to assert span emission for an SA, but this is OPTIONAL and only required when a feature spec mandates span observability (none of F13/F14/F16 do). Skip the test layer unless an SA explicitly needs it.

13. **M1/M2/M3 mutation contract** — pre-baked in the RFR with file paths, exact patches, expected SA failures, and `git checkout --` restore commands. ONE mutation per feature (3 total across the bundle):
    M1 (F13): in `tests/integration/test_quality_pipeline.rs`, change the seeded issues' severity in `SeededG7Source::issues` from `Severity::Critical` for the highest-severity issue to `Severity::Info`. Expected: SA2 fails (severity vocabulary check still passes since `Info` is in vocab — REVISE) — actually M1 should mutate the daemon-side severity merge logic. Better: M1 (F13) targets `crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs:handle_check_quality` — drop the `g7_outcome.results` join (replicate WO-0090 M1) — but that's daemon-side, not test-side. To keep mutations test-file-local: M1 (F13) changes the # of seeded issues from 5 to 1 in `SeededG7Source` so SA1 (≥ 3 issues) trips with `(SA1) issues[] length ≥ 3; left: 1, right: 3`. Restore: `git checkout -- tests/integration/test_quality_pipeline.rs`.
    M2 (F14): in `tests/integration/test_testing_pipeline.rs`, change `SeededG8Source::method` from `TestDiscoveryMethod::Convention` to `TestDiscoveryMethod::Import` for one of the seeded sources. Expected: SA3 fails (`methods_found_by` no longer covers all 3 methods). Restore: `git checkout --`.
    M3 (F16a): in `tests/integration/test_query_pipeline.rs`, drop the `with_g4_sources(...)` builder call from the McpServer construction. Expected: SA1 fails (`_meta.g4_fused` missing). Restore: `git checkout --`.
    The Verifier applies via `Edit` (in-place, no `git stash`), takes `md5sum` snapshot before any mutation, restores via `git checkout --`, confirms md5 match. WO-0067/0068/0069/0070/0085/0089/0090/0093 precedent.

14. **`scripts/verify/P3-W11-F13.sh` / `P3-W11-F14.sh` / `P3-W11-F16.sh`** — three NEW verify scripts, one per feature, each shape: shebang `#!/usr/bin/env bash`, `set -euo pipefail`, run `cargo test --test <test_name> 2>&1 | tee /tmp/p3w11f<NN>.log` and assert via `grep -qE 'test result: ok\.' /tmp/p3w11f<NN>.log`. F16 script invokes BOTH `--test test_query_pipeline --test test_fusion` per the feature-list selector. Mark all three `chmod +x` in the same commit. Mirror `scripts/verify/P3-W10-F13.sh` (WO-0093) shape verbatim minus the `pub mod` grep (no module declaration to verify here — the [[test]] entry in Cargo.toml is the equivalent).

16. **Each test wrapped in `tokio::time::timeout(Duration::from_secs(30), ...)`** to bound runtime. The `serve()` loop has its own per-request timeout via the daemon's per-source-deadline machinery; the wall-clock timeout here is a safety net against a stuck-task hang under CI.

17. **Test naming convention**: each `pub async fn test_*` name should match a recognizable selector substring per DEC-0007 + WO-0067/0068/0069/0070/0083/0085 precedent. Suggested names:
    - F13: `test_quality_pipeline_detects_severity_classified_issues`
    - F14: `test_testing_pipeline_discovers_tests_in_fixture_projects`
    - F16a: `test_query_pipeline_returns_fused_results_with_group_provenance`
    - F16b (test_fusion.rs may have multiple test fns):
        - `test_fusion_g2_rrf_correctness`
        - `test_fusion_cross_group_dedup_and_provenance`
        - `test_fusion_g1_with_partial_source_coverage`
    Each with the SAn panic-message body convention from WO-0067 / DEC-0007.

18. **Fixture data is INLINE, not external files**: each test seeds its golden inputs as inline `vec![...]` literals or `serde_json::json!(...)` bodies inside the test fn. NO new files under `tests/fixtures/**` (forbidden_paths) — the existing `mixed-project`, `rust-project`, `python-project`, `typescript-project`, `python_project` fixtures are read-only and only relevant if a test exercises file-watcher / TreeSitter ingest paths (none of F13/F14/F16 do — they all seed at the G-source trait level).

19. **DO NOT enable feature-gated coverage measurement** — these are integration tests (cross-crate); `cargo llvm-cov --package ucil-tests-integration` does not give meaningful per-crate coverage for the daemon's source. Coverage is measured per-source-crate (ucil-daemon, ucil-core, etc.) via the existing AC23 standing-protocol substantive-coverage check. Integration tests' role is to exercise the public API surface, not to bump per-crate line coverage.

20. **Word-ban scrub coverage**: pre-flight `! grep -qiE 'mock|fake|stub' tests/integration/test_quality_pipeline.rs tests/integration/test_testing_pipeline.rs tests/integration/test_query_pipeline.rs tests/integration/test_fusion.rs scripts/verify/P3-W11-F13.sh scripts/verify/P3-W11-F14.sh scripts/verify/P3-W11-F16.sh` MUST return empty for production-side files. The test-side `Seeded<G>Source` impl + (optional) tracing-subscriber test layer live under `#[cfg(test)]`-implicit (test files are compiled as test binaries; the entire file is `#[cfg(test)]`-equivalent) and are exempt under WO-0048 / WO-0093 precedent. Use POSITIVE phrasing in the rustdoc per WO-0093 scope_in #25.
21. **No `unsafe` blocks** per .claude/rules/rust-style.md §Unsafe. AC grep `! grep -qE 'unsafe\s*\{|unsafe\s+fn' tests/integration/test_*.rs` enforces.

22. **No `std::process::Command` in async paths** per .claude/rules/rust-style.md §Async (`tokio::process::Command` only). The integration tests do NOT spawn subprocesses (they use in-process duplex); this is preventive.

23. **Module-coherence per DEC-0005**: each integration test file is its own coherent commit unit; bundling all 4 files into ONE commit is acceptable per the WO-0067 ceqp.rs (548 LOC) / WO-0068 cross_group.rs (788 LOC) / WO-0089 g8.rs (~700 LOC) / WO-0093 agent_scheduler.rs (~1858 LOC) precedent — though splitting per-file is also acceptable since each test file is a self-contained compile unit. Prefer 4 separate `feat` commits (one per test file) for clean review chunks; bundle Cargo.toml updates with the FIRST `feat` commit so the workspace builds incrementally.

24. **Suggested commit budget** (NOT binding — adapt as needed): 7 commits.
    (1) `build(tests-integration): add four [[test]] entries for W11 pipeline integration tests` — Cargo.toml updates only.
    (2) `test(integration): add test_fusion.rs covering RRF + cross-group fusion (P3-W11-F16)` — F16b only.
    (3) `test(integration): add test_query_pipeline.rs full-pipeline golden assertions (P3-W11-F16)` — F16a only.
    (4) `test(integration): add test_quality_pipeline.rs check_quality severity assertions (P3-W11-F13)` — F13.
    (5) `test(integration): add test_testing_pipeline.rs run_tests discovery assertions (P3-W11-F14)` — F14.
    (6) `build(scripts): add three verify shell scripts for P3-W11-F13/F14/F16` — verify scripts only.
    (7) `chore(work-orders): WO-0094 ready for review` — RFR marker.

25. **Public surface dependencies — verify via `cargo metadata`**: confirm at executor-time that `ucil_daemon::g7::{G7Source, G7Query, G7Issue, G7SourceOutput, G7SourceStatus, Severity}`, `ucil_daemon::g8::{G8Source, G8Query, G8SourceOutput, G8SourceStatus, G8TestCandidate, TestDiscoveryMethod}`, `ucil_daemon::server::McpServer`, `ucil_daemon::executor::{fuse_g1, G1Outcome, G1FusionEntry, ...}`, `ucil_core::fusion::{fuse_g2_rrf, G2Hit, G2Source, G2SourceResults, G2FusedOutcome}`, `ucil_core::cross_group::{fuse_cross_group, Group, GroupHit, GroupResult, CrossGroupQuery, CrossGroupFusedOutcome}` are all PUB-reachable via the existing `pub mod` / `pub use` declarations. Pre-flight confirmed at planner-time: `g7` and `g8` are `pub mod` in `crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs:162-163` (their internal types are pub but not re-exported at lib root); `executor`, `server`, and `lifecycle` are explicitly re-exported via `pub use`; `cross_group` and `fusion` are pub-reachable from `ucil-core`. The integration tests use the explicit-module path (`ucil_daemon::g7::G7Source`) — no new `pub use` re-exports are required (and adding them is OUT OF SCOPE per the forbidden_paths constraint on `crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs`).

26. **No new EXTERNAL workspace deps** (per .claude/rules/rust-style.md §Crate layout). The existing `tokio`, `async-trait`, `lsp-types`, `serde_json`, `tempfile` in `tests/integration/Cargo.toml` cover all needs. If a test fn needs a new dep, EITHER: (a) author an ADR justifying the addition; OR (b) hand-roll equivalent functionality in <30 LOC per WO-0067 `extract_gaps` / WO-0093 Jaccard-similarity precedent.

27. **Three pre-existing flake escalations carry as standing scope_out**: `20260507T0357Z-effectiveness-nav-rust-symbol-rs-line-flake.md`, `20260507T1629Z-effectiveness-refactor-rename-python-fixture-missing-symbol.md`, `20260507T1930Z-effectiveness-nav-rust-symbol-doctest-caller-flake.md` — `scripts/verify/effectiveness-gate.sh` claude-p sub-session timeout flake is NOT this WO's responsibility per WO-0067/0068/0069/0090/0093 precedent. Verifier may skip the effectiveness-gate.sh step if the sub-session times out; the substantive AC is the `cargo test --test test_*` invocations which are deterministic.

28. **Integration-test-suite mutation contract simplification**: unlike backbone WOs (G7/G8/warm-processors) where M1/M2/M3 target the SAME source file (atomic restore), this bundle's mutations target THREE DIFFERENT files (test_quality_pipeline.rs, test_testing_pipeline.rs, test_query_pipeline.rs). Verifier MUST take a `md5sum` snapshot of each file BEFORE applying any mutation, restore each file independently via `git checkout -- <file>`, and confirm md5 match per-file. This is a NEW pattern — document it in the RFR's 'Mutation contract' section. The `reality-check.sh` step 6b stash interaction (informational-only per WO-0093 lessons §verifier) carries forward unchanged.
scope_out: 1. **F11 W9 incremental test (`test_incremental.rs`)** — different consumer axis (Salsa+watcher, not MCP-pipeline); subsequent WO. WO-0090 / WO-0091 / WO-0092 scope_out's 'Subsequent WO bundles 2-4 of these' permits 1-test or 2-test bundles in the next WO if the planner so decides.

2. **F12 W11 feedback-loop post-hoc analyser** — different crate (`ucil-core`, not `tests/integration/`); different architectural axis (post-response state mutation, importance score boost/decay); subsequent WO. Selector `-p ucil-core feedback::` is unrelated to the integration-test selectors.

3. **G3/G4/G5/G6/G7/G8 production-source wiring** — real `LspDiagnosticsG7Source` / `EslintG7Source` / `RuffG7Source` / `SemgrepG7Source` / `ConventionTestG8Source` / `ImportTestG8Source` / `KgRelationsTestG8Source` / `AiderRepoMapG5Source` / `Context7G5Source` / `RepomixG5Source` / `GitHubG6Source` / `FilesystemG6Source` / `GitG6Source` impls into `lifecycle.rs` boot-time wiring + `McpServer::with_default_g{N}_sources()` factory — carried-forward from WO-0070/0083/0085/0089/0090/0091. The integration tests inject test-side `Seeded<G>Source` per the validated DEC-0008 §4 trait-seam pattern; production wiring is a separate eligible bundle.

4. **F13 production-source wiring within this WO**: do NOT add `lifecycle.rs::McpServer::with_default_g7_g8_sources()` factory or production-source impl files in this WO; that is the F10/F15 production-wiring follow-up (carried from WO-0090). Stay strictly within `tests/integration/` + `tests/integration/Cargo.toml` + `scripts/verify/P3-W11-F13/F14/F16.sh` + this WO's RFR.

5. **F14 `run_tests` actual subprocess execution**: do NOT spawn real `test-runner-mcp` / `mcp-pytest-runner` subprocesses in the integration test. The `Seeded G8Source` test-side impl returns deterministic candidates without subprocess invocation. Real-subprocess test-runner integration is covered by WO-0082 (test-runner-mcp-revival, P3-W11-F07) at the plugin-manifest layer; cross-tier integration with subprocess execution is a separate follow-up.

6. **F16 cross-group fusion against real Aider repo-map / Context7 / Repomix output**: do NOT bring up real subprocess sources for G3/G4/G5/G6 in this WO. The integration tests inject `Seeded<G>Source` impls for each group. Production wiring is the eligible follow-up per scope_out #3.

7. **Modifications to `crates/ucil-daemon/src/**`**: no source files under `crates/ucil-daemon/src/` are touched in this WO. The McpServer's existing public builder API (validated through WO-0083/0085/0089/0090/0093) is sufficient. If a test requires a new builder method or a new pub re-export, AUTHOR AN ADR proposing the addition and DEFER the WO until the ADR is accepted.

8. **Modifications to `crates/ucil-core/src/**`**: same constraint — out of scope. The `fusion::fuse_g2_rrf` and `cross_group::fuse_cross_group` and `executor::fuse_g1` public functions are sufficient for `test_fusion.rs`.
9. **Modifications to `tests/fixtures/**`**: forbidden_paths. The fixtures are read-only; tests may READ them via `Path::new("tests/fixtures/<name>")` but must NOT modify them.

11. **Modifications to `ucil-build/feature-list.json`**: only the verifier's `flip-feature.sh` invocation may change `passes` etc. The planner does NOT mutate feature-list.json beyond the existing seed.

12. **Modifications to the master plan (`ucil-master-plan-v2.1-final.md`)**: forbidden — the spec is immutable. Any conflict triggers an ADR (`ucil-build/decisions/proposed-*.md`), not a master-plan edit.

13. **Verifier-side `effectiveness-gate.sh` timeout flake** — pre-existing (3 escalations); standing scope_out per WO-0067/68/69/90/93 precedent. Verifier may skip this step when the claude-p sub-session times out.

14. **Coverage-gate.sh sccache `RUSTC_WRAPPER` workaround** — standing carry-forward (now 45+ WOs deep through WO-0093). Substantive AC23 measure via `env -u RUSTC_WRAPPER cargo llvm-cov --package <crate> --summary-only --json | jq '.data[0].totals.lines.percent'` is the binding floor.

lessons_applied: WO-0090 §planner — 'integration test suites P3-W9-F11, P3-W11-F13/14/16 — different architectural axis (additive tests/integration/test_*.rs files using docker fixtures). Subsequent WO bundles 2-4 of these together per the test-suite-bundle template.' Applied: bundle 3 (F13+F14+F16) sharing the W11 MCP-pipeline + tier_merger axis; F11 (W9 incremental, Salsa+watcher axis) deferred.

WO-0091 §planner — 'feedback loop post-hoc analyser (P3-W11-F12), warm processors (P3-W10-F13), and the four phase-3 integration test suites (P3-W9-F11, P3-W11-F13/14/16) — different architectural axes. Eligible for separate WOs.' Applied: F12 deferred to a separate WO.

WO-0092 §planner — 'integration test suites P3-W9-F11 (incremental), P3-W11-F13 (quality pipeline), P3-W11-F14 (testing pipeline), P3-W11-F16 (full query pipeline) — different architectural axis (additive tests/integration/test_*.rs files using docker fixtures). Subsequent WO bundles 2-4 of these per the test-suite-bundle template.' Applied: bundle of 3 W11 features.

WO-0093 §planner — 'F11/F14/F16 integration test suites: depend on F13 transitively per scope_out (F11/F14/F16 are integration test suites that consume F13's outputs). Now eligible for emission post-WO-0093 — bundle-coordinated planner emission likely (3 integration tests sharing the docker-fixture + tier_merger consumer axis).' Applied: bundle of 3 (note: WO-0093's reference 'F11/F14/F16' was a typo — F11 is the review_changes MCP tool covered by WO-0092; the correct integration-test trio is F13/F14/F16, which is what this WO bundles).

WO-0093 §planner — 'scope_in #25 forbidden-pattern declaration phrasing — when prescribing the module-header rustdoc statement of forbidden patterns, AVOID literal trigger words mock|fake|stub even in the negation. The ! grep -qiE pattern is case-INsensitive and matches inside rustdoc.' Applied: scope_in #9 prescribes POSITIVE phrasing ('the trait is the dep-inversion seam — production impls live in crates/ucil-daemon/').

WO-0090 §planner — 'AC selectors must tolerate rustfmt macro-arg wrapping. Avoid literal single-line greps for any expression that rustfmt may wrap.' Applied: ACs use `cargo test ... 2>&1 | grep -qE 'test result: ok\.'` rather than literal source-line greps for assertions; mutation contract uses verbatim file paths + `git checkout --` restore rather than line-number-pinned diffs.

WO-0090 §planner — 'scope_out dep-claims must be verified at planner-time'. Applied: verified at planner-time (via `cargo metadata` reasoning + Read on `tests/integration/Cargo.toml`) that the existing dev-deps `tokio`, `async-trait`, `lsp-types`, `serde_json`, `tempfile`, `ucil-core`, `ucil-daemon`, `ucil-lsp-diagnostics` are sufficient for all four new test files. NO new external workspace deps prescribed (per scope_in #4).

WO-0089/0090/0093 §verifier — 'AC --list separator standing carry-forward'. Applied: scope_in #14 + AC19 + AC20 specify modern `cargo test ... 2>&1 | grep` form (no `--list` since the ACs run cargo test directly, not list-mode).

WO-0067 §planner — 'WOs that bundle two coordinated features around a shared master-plan-§N.M contract SHOULD pre-bake one mutation per feature in scope_in[].acceptance naming the specific assertion that should fire and the restore command.' Applied: scope_in #13 + AC30/31/32 pre-bake M1/M2/M3 with file paths, exact patches, expected SAn panic-body, and `git checkout --` restore — one mutation per feature in the 3-feature bundle.

WO-0093 §planner — 'G-backbone template SEPTUPLE-validation status — when no peer features share the thematic invariant; ~1800 LOC scope is appropriate for trait+orchestration+frozen-test bundles.' Adapted: for integration-test bundles (different shape than backbone WOs), prescribe ~1000-1300 LOC across 4 files in 6-8 commits per scope_in #23/24, reflecting the per-test-file independence.

WO-0083/0085/0089/0090/0093 — 'Test-side dependency-inversion seam carry-forward depth'. Applied: each integration test file declares its own `Seeded<G>Source` impl per the trait-seam pattern; no test helpers shared across files (each file is a self-contained compile unit per the [[test]] entry in Cargo.toml).

WO-0093 §verifier — 'reality-check.sh same-file pre-existing-stash bug is informational only; the M1/M2/M3 in-place mutation contract with git checkout -- + md5sum verify is authoritative.' Applied: scope_in #28 + AC33 explicitly mandate per-file md5sum snapshot + per-file `git checkout --` restore for the 3 mutations across 3 files (NOT a single-file mutation contract like prior backbone WOs).
